rendererContract.renderer: isolated official MaterialX ESSL/FIS RawShaderMaterial backend; Three TSL remains available with ?implementation=tsl
rendererContract.environment: materialx/references/studio-environment.exr FIS radiance plus studio-irradiance.exr SH diffuse irradiance at 0.18 intensity

bakedPbrFallback.scope: 64 x 32 probe sphere UV topology; production assets require their own bake UVs

recoveredSemantics: ShaderNodeTexCoord.Generated is reconstructed from object-space position and evaluated bounds with a zero-extent guard.
ShaderNodeAttribute rough is restored as geompropvalue from the exact 2.5D Chrome Crayon FACE-domain contract and flat-expanded vertex binding.
remainingRendererLimitations: Three 0.185.1 TSL still rejects geompropvalue; the isolated official ESSL backend supports the recovered graph.

capabilityGate.reason: native extraction, ESSL generation, live 2.5D binding, and matched capture pass; authored chrome remains the default because zero-roughness Eevee and MaterialX FIS highlights still have substantial foreground pixel differences

requestedNoiseWaveBumpAudit.sourceMaterialDiscrepancy: The supplied chrome.003 graph contains Noise but no Wave or Bump. The Noise/Bump material on the lab is an explicitly labeled compatibility probe, not a claim about the absent source nodes.
threeNativeUnsupported: Procedural heighttonormal semantics (the r185 implementation samples TextureNode UVs only)
requestedNoiseWaveBumpAudit.prototypeExtension: A general TSL adapter recognizes heighttonormal -> normalmap, ports the MaterialX 1.39 Sobel and world tangent-frame semantics, and handles supported procedural math/noise nodes.
requestedNoiseWaveBumpAudit.fallback: Wave, named-attribute, and failed-preflight graphs resolve to baked-pbr when supplied, otherwise legacy-authored.

substituted: world normal neutralized on identity probe
